item.title: Bruce Springsteen’s Anti-Trump Message Isn’t Hurting Business
item.description: The New Jersey legend crossed the US on an anti-Trump tour and released a protest song. His fans keep buying tickets and listening.

item.title: Chile’s Top Lawmaker Says Debt Hike to Pass at Cost to Prestige
item.description: Chile’s new government is risking the nation’s hard-won reputation for fiscal prudence by seeking permission to sell billions of dollars in extra debt this year, the lower house speaker said.

item.title: Airbnb CEO Brian Chesky Plans to Start a New AI Company
item.description: Airbnb Inc. Chief Executive Officer Brian Chesky is starting a new artificial intelligence lab, according to several people familiar with the matter, marking his first foray into the global AI race.

item.title: AI Power Boom Revives Natural Gas as Infrastructure Bet
item.description: A surge in electricity demand from artificial intelligence and data centers is reshaping North America’s power markets, creating a “renaissance” for natural gas after decades of limited new development, according to KKR & Co. partner Brandon Freiman.

item.title: LS Power Said to Near Deal For EDF’s American Renewables Arm
item.description: LS Power LLC is in advanced talks to buy Electricite de France SA’s North American renewable power business amid rising energy demand to supply data centers, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Muni Tobacco Bonds Have First-Ever Default as Smoking Declines
item.description: The municipal bond market’s $80 billion tobacco bond sector had its first-ever default after a Nassau County, New York, agency failed to make a $36 million principal payment on June 1.

item.title: Easy Money With an Uneasy Economy
item.description: Markets are flying, businesses feel less sure.

item.title: Court Blocks Order for US Official to Testify on Tariff Refunds
item.description: A federal appeals court has for now suspended a judge’s order to compel Customs and Border Protection Commissioner Rodney Scott to testify about the Trump administration’s efforts to refund $166 billion in tariffs declared unlawful by the US Supreme Court earlier this year.

item.title: Shark Tank Star Shrinks Data Center Footprint After Backlash
item.description: A proposed Utah data center that would have been almost three times the size of Manhattan will be drastically scaled back after pressure from lawmakers.

item.title: Hatch’s New Bedside Sleep Clock Wirelessly Tracks Sleep Quality
item.description: Hatch Baby Inc., known for devices that help babies sleep, is getting into sleep-tracking for adults with a new bedside tracker called the Hatch Sleep Clock.

item.title: Jane Street Plans New Data Center as Compute Power Runs Scarce
item.description: Jane Street Group plans to build and finance its own data center as the trading giant scales its operations to keep up with the demand for computing power.

item.title: AI Isn’t Replacing Credit Hedge Fund Traders Yet, Barclays Says
item.description: Hedge funds and asset managers are increasingly using artificial intelligence when investing in global credit markets, but the technology isn’t replacing human traders — at least for now, according to a survey by Barclays Plc.

item.title: Anthropic President Cites High Computing Costs as Driver for IPO
item.description: Anthropic PBC co-founder and President Daniela Amodei said the high cost of developing artificial intelligence models is driving firms like hers to look to the public market for capital, days after the Claude maker confidentially filed paperwork for an IPO.

item.title: Honeywell-Backed Quantinuum Shares Jump 13% After Upsized IPO
item.description: Quantinuum Inc., a quantum computing company backed by Honeywell International Inc., opened 13% above its listing price after raising $1.68 billion in an upsized US initial public offering.

item.title: Power Equipment Firm Innio Rises 15% After $2.43 Billion IPO
item.description: Shares of Innio Holding GmbH gained 15% after the gas engine maker’s biggest shareholders raised $2.43 billion in an initial public offering.

item.title: Daly Says Fed Ready to Respond ‘Either Way’ With Interest Rates
item.description: Federal Reserve Bank of San Francisco President Mary Daly said monetary policy is in a good place right now, but there’s too much uncertainty in the economy to offer a view on where rates are headed.

item.title: Progress in US-Iran Talks Stalls After Hezbollah Rejects Truce
item.description: There was no sign of progress in ceasefire talks between the US and Iran after the worst burst of violence in weeks and as the Tehran-backed Hezbollah militia rejected on Thursday a US-brokered truce in Lebanon.

item.title: Michael Saylor’s Bitcoin Machine Is Misfiring on Every Cylinder
item.description: Michael Saylor built Strategy Inc. around a straightforward idea: raise money to buy Bitcoin, then never sell it. Over time, that simple bet has turned far more complicated.

item.title: Jack Dorsey’s Contactless Payments Company Introduces a Wand
item.description: In the era of tap-to-pay, Block Inc. is trying to make things a little more enchanting.

item.title: Oil Shocks Have Smaller Impact on US Than 1970s, Fed Study Finds
item.description: Domestic production of oil has significantly reduced the impact of energy-price shocks on US inflation and unemployment since the 1970s, according to new research from the Federal Reserve Bank of Boston.

item.title: Databricks CEO Plans to Avoid IPO During Year of Huge Offerings
item.description: Databricks, a data software company, plans to sell shares to the public at some point, but won’t tap the public markets this year amid a flurry of planned tech IPOs.

item.title: Active Stock Funds Reel as Big Tech’s Grip on Market Strengthens
item.description: Active stock pickers are finding it harder to stomach the pain of technology megacaps’ strengthening grip on the stock market.

item.title: Sunshine Silver Mining Rises 11% After $270 Million US IPO
item.description: Sunshine Silver Mining & Refining Co. shares opened 11% above their IPO price after the mine owner raised $270 million in an upsized US initial public offering.

item.title: Anthropic’s Daniela Amodei Speaks at Bloomberg Tech
item.description: The downtown skyline of San Francisco. Photographer: Jason Henry/Bloomberg

item.title: Blackstone-Backed Liftoff Rises 9.1% After $437 Million US IPO
item.description: Liftoff Mobile Inc. shares climbed 21% in their debut after raising $437 million in a US initial public offering, in the company’s second attempt at going public this year.

item.title: Dangote Is Doubling Giant Oil Refinery and Plotting Trading Push
item.description: Aliko Dangote has started work to double capacity at his huge oil refinery in Nigeria, which when finished will rival the world’s largest site and give Africa’s richest person a stronger grip of global fuel supply chains.

item.title: Treasuries Rise as Oil Signals Optimism on Iran Peace Accord
item.description: Treasuries rose and the dollar slipped as investors drew optimism about the prospects of a US-Iran peace deal from a conditional ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon, despite ongoing clashes.

item.title: Morgan Stanley Sees Fed Discounting War in Considering Any Hike
item.description: The Federal Reserve would probably discount the effects of the Iran war on prices if policymakers decide raising interest rates becomes necessary this year, according to Morgan Stanley’s global head of fixed income research.

item.title: Canada to Provide Funding, Buy Equity Stakes in AI Startups
item.description: Canada will provide funding to help artificial intelligence companies scale up, and plans to take equity stakes in the country’s most promising AI firms to accelerate the creation of so-called “national champions”.

item.title: How Funding Cuts Left the World Vulnerable to Ebola
item.description: A strain with no known treatment or vaccine is spreading fast, and the international system built to contain outbreaks is in crisis.
